title: RVLV Q1 Deep Dive: Brand Investments, Category Expansion, and Input Cost Pressures Shape Results
text: Revolve Group's Q1 2026 results demonstrate the ongoing tension between growth investments and margin expansion. Brand investments including the REVOLVE Los Angeles launch and Cardi B Grow-Good Beauty partnership are driving customer acquisition and brand awareness but requiring significant marketing spend. The company maintained marketing investments at approximately 15.7% of net sales in Q2 guidance and 15.3%-15.8% for full-year 2026, slightly above prior guidance, reflecting the strategic decision to invest during this growth phase. Category expansion initiatives in beauty, men's fashion, and home goods contributed to the 16% revenue growth, with beauty showing particular strength. However, input cost pressures on both freight and materials are impacting gross margins, particularly affecting the REVOLVE segment. CFO Jesse Timmermans noted the headwinds from higher freight and materials costs affecting margin expansion, though the company maintained positive gross margin guidance of 53.5%-54.0% for 2026. The Middle East's meaningful geopolitical slowdown represents a near-term headwind, though management expects normalization as the year progresses. Overall, the quarter reflects a company balancing near-term margin pressure with longer-term growth investments.
